title: Latency, Throughput, Memory, Caching, Load, and Cost · Language Clinic: Rag Load Fault Capacity Test
summary: A team optimizes average response time with a warm cache, ignores index memory and model queueing, retrieves more candidates on every request, and reports token price without total service cost. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the load fault capacity test increment in RAG capacity and cost envelope. Learners…
objectives: Test realistic query mixes, cold and warm state, filter selectivity, update load, provider limits, degraded dependencies, quality, recovery, and cost together.
Account for ingestion, parsing, OCR, embeddings, storage, index build, query, reranking, generation, network, observability, evaluation, operations, and recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-performance-cost-capacity-3
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-performance-cost-capacity-3.
# Case 5798: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def worked_rag_performance_cost_capacity_3(queries_per_second: int = 24, retrieval_ms: int = 45, rerank_ms: int = 38, generation_ms: int = 420, deadline_ms: int = 650, cost_microunits: int = 950, cache_hit: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "query3dekrow",
        "tenant5798",
        "indexworked35798",
        "prompt3dekrow5798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if queries_per_second <= 0 or min(retrieval_ms, rerank_ms, generation_ms) < 0:
        return False, 0, "capacity-input"
    total_ms = retrieval_ms + rerank_ms + generation_ms
    if total_ms > deadline_ms:
        return False, total_ms, "latency-budget"
    if cost_microunits < 0:
        return False, cost_microunits, "cost-accounting"
    adjusted_cost = cost_microunits // 2 if cache_hit else cost_microunits
    assert adjusted_cost <= cost_microunits
    return True, adjusted_cost, "capacity-envelope"

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-load-fault-capacity-test-9d21ka" that uses case 7625 to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-load-fault-capacity-test-9d21ka
# Competency: rag-load-fault-capacity-test.
# Case 7625: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def evidence_rag_load_fault_capacity_test_9d21ka(queries_per_second: int = 24, retrieval_ms: int = 45, rerank_ms: int = 38, generation_ms: int = 420, deadline_ms: int = 650, cost_microunits: int = 950, cache_hit: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_9d21ka = "9d21ka-ak12d9-7625"
    assert evidence_variant_9d21ka
    evidence_axes_9d21ka = (
        "corpus9d21ka",
        "queryak12d9",
        "tenant7625",
        "index9d21ka7625",
        "promptak12d97625",
        "evaluation76259d21ka",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_9d21ka)) == 6
    if queries_per_second <= 0 or min(retrieval_ms, rerank_ms, generation_ms) < 0:
        return False, 0, "capacity-input"
    total_ms = retrieval_ms + rerank_ms + generation_ms
    if total_ms > deadline_ms:
        return False, total_ms, "latency-budget"
    if cost_microunits < 0:
        return False, cost_microunits, "cost-accounting"
    adjusted_cost = cost_microunits // 2 if cache_hit else cost_microunits
    assert adjusted_cost <= cost_microunits
    return True, adjusted_cost, "capacity-envelope"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Load Fault Capacity Test: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 7515 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test realistic query mixes, cold and warm state, filter selectivity, update load, provider limits, degraded dependencies, quality, recovery, and cost together.
